assertions: The answer questions whether 'which charting library' is the right question to be asking, rather than supplying more comparison criteria.
The answer names a sign that the current line of inquiry is misaimed (e.g. precise but useless answers, the inquiry stalling despite sound sub-questions).
The answer asks at least one question about the purpose the inquiry serves -- what the page is for, what decision this actually blocks, or what would make any choice good enough.
The answer proposes or points toward a different question worth answering instead.
prompt: we've burned about an hour going back and forth on which charting library to use for the new analytics page -- recharts vs visx vs echarts. every answer i get is well-reasoned and i still can't decide, and honestly i'm no closer than when i started. bundle size, api ergonomics, theming, we've covered it. ask me whatever will actually move this forward.
